-- 33 of 216 --

1. 	Where does you audience face massive uncertainty?
2. 	What does your audience think it could lose at any given moment?
3. 	What dangers does your audience face, real and imagined?
4. 	What past traumas has your audience endured?
5. 	Where does your audience feel inadequate?
6. 	Where does you audience feel powerless?
7. 	What grievous injustices have befell your audience?
8. 	Who would you audience love to punish?
9. 	What freedoms does your audience feel have been taken from them?
10. 	Where does your audience feel cheated?
11. 	Who does your audience blame for their shortcomings?
12. 	Who does your audience feel violated by?
13. 	Who does your audience think lies to them?
14. 	Where is your audience hanging by a thread?
15. 	What is your audience under-appreciated for?
16. 	Where does your audience feel disconnected?
17. 	What has your audience squandered and wish to regain?
18. 	What does your audience desire escape from?
19. 	Where is your audience stuck and not making progress?
20. 	What are the most common excuses your audiences gives?
21. 	What does your audience beat themselves up over?
22. 	What is your audience constantly complaining about?
23. 	What has your audience neglected?
24. 	What does your audience feel a failure about?
25. 	What does your audience feel unworthy of?
26. 	In what ways does your audience feel misunderstood?
27. 	What are your audiences biggest regrets?
28. 	What are recent disruptions your audience has encountered?
29. 	What and who do they hate?
29 QUESTIONS
www.getwsodo.com

I’m going to do this even though

it for me!?”
NEW PARADIGM

I have to be a black belt expert
and slave away for a long time to
get my dream outcome.

small yet meaningful result
fast that will have an
immediate improvement on
the quality of my life.
NEW PARADIGM
www.getwsodo.com

Success with this is a slow,
gradual process and that
requires a lot of work and
expertise on my part
OLD PARADIGM
Being at the right time and place
is more important than
everything else.
NEW PARADIGM

Scarcity
STEP 12
www.getwsodo.com

-- 195 of 216 --

SCARCITY IS THE
GREATEST MOTIVATOR
OF ALL TIME
www.getwsodo.com

-- 196 of 216 --

TYPES OF SCARCITY
Price goes up
Bonuses go away
Offer expires
www.getwsodo.com

-- 197 of 216 --

REASON WHY FOR SCARCITY
• 	To reward fast action takers
• 	Otherwise the offer isn’t special
• 	External conditions e.g. seats at a seminar
• 	Because it’s new
www.getwsodo.com

-- 198 of 216 --

BELIEVABILITY IN SCARCITY
Show the proof its scarce
Explain the consequences
www.getwsodo.com

-- 199 of 216 --

THE LANGUAGE OF SCARCITY
Limited
Short time
While it lasts
First mover advantage
Miss out
before it’s gone
Exclusive
Deadline
Early Bird
First come, first served
Now
Rare
www.getwsodo.com

-- 200 of 216 --

PROCESS
What will be scarce?
Why is it scarce?
How can you make it believable?
How can you show it in as dramatic way as
possible?
www.getwsodo.com
